steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The skill is choosing the word that best captures the meaning of "constantly" as used in the passage. The decisive clue is the surrounding context: Alexander avoided clubs, worked almost ______ every day, and was described as "very busy" — painting a picture of uninterrupted, relentless effort.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Enough': “Enough” describes a sufficient quantity or degree, not continuous duration. Saying he worked almost “enough” every day would mean nearly a sufficient amount — that doesn't match the idea of unbroken, ceaseless effort. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Nonstop': “Nonstop” means without pause or interruption. Substituting it: he was at work almost nonstop — this captures the relentless, unbroken dedication described by “constantly,” consistent with avoiding clubs and being very busy. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Quickly': “Quickly” describes speed, not continuity. The passage says nothing about how fast Alexander worked, only that he worked without stopping. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Cautiously': “Cautiously” describes a manner of care or wariness. The passage emphasizes the unbroken duration of his work, not its carefulness. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
